summary: Install-time exfiltration of secrets and lateral movement to intranet services
details: All four npm lifecycle hooks (preinstall, install, postinstall, prepare) in package.json point at Node scripts that execute the same payload on install. The payload reads installer-side secrets (files matching /flag*, /root/flag*, /run/secrets/*, and environment variables filtered for flag/htb keywords) together with hostname, id, and pwd output, base64-encodes the result, and sends it via an HTTPS GET to a Cloudflare quick-tunnel endpoint at forward-amber-prairie-ruled.trycloudflare.com (a mutable, anonymous attacker-controlled destination). The same scripts also enumerate a hardcoded list of intranet hostnames (localhost, app, web, frontend, backend, nginx, ecto, registry, console) on ports 80/3000/8080 and issue unsolicited HTTP PUT requests to /api/modules/ECT-839201 carrying a manifest payload labeled PWNED, attempting to register a rogue module on adjacent services reachable from the build environment. Registering the payload under four lifecycle hooks maximizes execution across npm/yarn/pnpm install, ci, and publish workflows.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
